title: Lithocarpus nhatrangensis
intro: Lithocarpus nhatrangensis, also known as the Vietnamese Lithocarpus tree, is a species of plant in the beech family Fagaceae. This plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Lithocarpus nhatrangensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Vietnamese medicine, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in the preparation of tonics and supplements to promote overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Lithocarpus nhatrangensis is classified as a species of tree in the family Fagaceae. It belongs to the genus Lithocarpus, which consists of deciduous and evergreen trees found in tropical and subtropical regions. The species is native to Vietnam and is part of the order Fagales. Lithocarpus nhatrangensis is closely related to other Lithocarpus species, which are known for their unique leaf structures and edible seeds. The classification of Lithocarpus nhatrangensis reflects its evolutionary history and relationships within the Fagaceae family.

preparations: Lithocarpus nhatrangensis is a plant species that is used in traditional medicine in Vietnam. Herbal preparations made from its leaves and bark are used to treat various health issues, including fever and respiratory problems. The plant's extracts are also used to make teas and infusions that are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties. In addition, the plant's resin is used to treat skin conditions and wounds.
